rulings.Case Title: ESTATE OF FLORENCE JEAN OW YEONG
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing, although the personal representative's capacity was shown on the Notice of Hearing (and in the Waiver of Account) as "Executor" when he is the successor ADMINISTRATOR, who was appointed on 5/24/23, not 5/25/23, and Letters were issued to him on 5/26/23, not 5/25/23, and the I&A was filed on 9/6/23 (although not e-filed until 9/7/23). In light of the former administrator's failure to timely close the estate, the Court will waive any right he or his estate might have had to statutory compensation. (NB: When papers are not filed electronically, each paper presented for filing must contain two prepunched normal-sized holes, centered 2-1/2 inches apart and 5/8 inch from the top of the paper. [CRC 2.115.] This includes proposed orders. An order IS proposed until it is signed; there is no need to caption the document, "[Proposed] Order.")

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF DAVID K BALL
rulings.Calendar Matter: PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 03/18/2024, as nothing has been filed to address the following issues (e-mailed to counsel on 12/27/2023 - see also tentative rulings for 12/06/2023 & 01/17/2024 hearings): 1) No additional inventory and appraisal has been filed since the partial one only listing small cash was filed in March 2023. 2) Has the petitioner taken possession of all the securities appraised by the referee in June 2022? If not, please describe efforts the petitioner has made and other updates since her supplemental declaration filed in February 2023. 3) Please provide an itemized list of assets escheated to the state and sought to be recovered. 4) Has the petitioner taken any IAEA actions reportable under CRC 7.250(a)? 5) Each status report must set forth all the information required by LRSF 14.35(J)(1), even if it was included in previous reports. Allegations re notice under Probate Code section 9202 and assignments or powers of attorney that may affect distribution must be provided. Note: An appearance may be required on 03/18/2024 if papers showing satisfactory progress to address the issues are not filed.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JOHN L. VENTURA
rulings.Calendar Matter: PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 3/18/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) No proposed order has been submitted or lodged. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] (2) No proof of service of notice has been filed. (3) Notice must include the procedures for making a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] Deadline is 3/8/24 for providing the assigned examiner a proposed order and courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. If, as the executor alleges in this petition, the estate is ready to close by 2/29/24 and a petition for final distribution is filed by then (or before 3/18/24), this petition will be taken off calendar on 3/18/24. (NB: Item 4 of an I&A should NOT be completed when a probate referee HAS BEEN appointed.)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF IVY BEATRICE CARO
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 3/18/2024. The following issues to be cleared two weeks prior: (1) An amended petition or an amendment to the petition needs to be filed to ensure the petition seeks Lost Will be admitted. A supplement was filed addressing related information; however, a supplement seems inappropriate to change the relief. CRC 7.3. LRSF 14.20. (2) Also, a new Notice of Petition to Administer Estate [DE-121] on the document mentioned in (1) needs to be provided to all persons. CRC 7.53. (3) Proof of Subscribing Witness was filed on 12/19/23; however, the declarant failed to date. (4) An additional hard copy of the lost will must be provided to the clerk for a will file to be created before the hearing to admit the lost will to probate. LRSF 14.20. (5) Petition #8 (Attachment 8) to identify each second generation's parent. Said information was attached to Notice of Hearing filed 1/16/24; however, the information was not verified by the petitioner. When petitioner prepares the document in (1), the information needs to be included. (6) Need further supporting allegation, if available, concerning that the original will was not in the decedent's possession. Appears petitioner merely alleges what he does not believe. Appears insufficient. (7) Petition #5.a. (3)-(4), re: surviving registered domestic partner, to be completed (8) Petition #5.a. (7)-(8), re: issue of a predeceased child, to be completed (9) Proposed Order must be submitted. LRSF 14.7.A. A copy of the purported lost will must be attached to the order. Prob. Code sec. 8223.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF THELMA F. BULLOCK
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The following issues to be cleared/addressed: 1. Need the notice to be given to the alternate executor - the Trust Officer of the Central CA Conference Association of the Seventh-Day Adventists. 2. Need Notice of Direction for Remote Appearances and Access to be served with the notice. LRSF 14.1.C.6). 3. Duties and Liabilities form includes the petitioner's mailing address (P.O. Box) only, not street address. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JUNGCHI SHIH
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 3/18/2024 at the counsel's consent. The following issues to be cleared seven days prior: (1) Proof of publication. (2) Need Notice of Direction for Remote Appearances and Access to be served with the notice to Ms. Li-Hua Shih Lin (mother). LRSF 14.1.C.6). (3) Waiver of Bond from Ms. Li-Hua Shih Lin. The petition conclusively alleges that the surviving spouse/petitioner is the sole successor-in-interest as the assets are all community property. However, the court has not determined the character of the assets, and it is not clear why this petition is necessary if all assets are community property. (4) Petition #5.a. (3)-(4) to be completed.
